etymology.description: ŔĘÇ(ŔĘÇ) (y├ín, "speech") + ŠĽľ(ŠĽľ) (├ío, "to go") = speech element + phonetic ŠĽľ
etymology.interpretation: The character ŔČĚ(ŔČĚ) (├ío) combines ŔĘÇ(ŔĘÇ) (y├ín, "speech"), which indicates the category of words related to speech or language, with ŠĽľ(ŠĽľ) (├ío, "to go"), serving as the phonetic component. It is not commonly used in contemporary Chinese, serving mainly as a phonetic element.
